tasks.title: Raise stall threshold and verify slowing window
tasks.description:
Context: Implement OpenSpec change rally-083-polish tasks 2.1-2.3. Read proposal.md, design.md, specs/relay-runner/spec.md, and tasks.md.

Outcome: Unset stall_threshold_secs defaults to 900 seconds, the monitor slowing indicator remains derived at 0.6x, and reliability.DefaultStallThreshold remains unchanged.

Files & scope: Focus on internal/config/config_v2.go, internal/monitor/monitor.go, and their existing tests. Document the accepted global-15m/opencode-idle trade-off in the config comment.

Acceptance: Add or update focused tests for the config default and slowing window, run relevant Go tests, and mark tasks 2.1-2.3 complete in tasks.md.

tasks.title: Harden OpenCode JSON parsing and fallback summaries
tasks.description:
Context: Implement OpenSpec change rally-083-polish tasks 3.1-3.2 using spike-opencode-json.md and spike-evidence/ as the schema authority. Read specs/executor/spec.md and design.md.

Outcome: parseOpenCodeOutput never emits raw stdout as Summary, parses ordered text/tool/step_finish events, handles top-level error events, and uses short bounded failure indicators.

Files & scope: Focus on internal/agent/opencode.go and internal/agent tests. Do not add early process reaping or per-harness stall behavior.

Design: Completion requires exit status semantics plus text or step_finish and no error event. Error summaries should use message/ref/name without transcript leakage.

Acceptance: Add tests based on captured event families, run relevant Go tests, and mark tasks 3.1-3.2 complete in tasks.md.

tasks.title: Normalize runner final snippets across retry and progress records
tasks.description:
Context: Implement OpenSpec change rally-083-polish tasks 3.3-3.5. Read design.md and specs/relay-runner/spec.md after the OpenCode parser lap.

Outcome: The runner uses laps wrapup summary as the golden source when available, otherwise executor final text, otherwise a bounded fallback; retry context, tries.jsonl, and summary.jsonl agree on the normalized snippet.

Files & scope: Focus on internal/relay runner flow and existing store/progress integration points. Preserve append-only summary.jsonl behavior.

Design: Keep one explicit normalization path and avoid storing raw transcripts or start-of-run narration.

Acceptance: Add focused tests for wrapup precedence, executor fallback, bounded fallback, and persisted/retry-context consistency; run relevant Go tests; mark tasks 3.3-3.5 complete in tasks.md.

tasks.title: Cap persisted final-snippet fields at 3000 runes
tasks.description:
Context: Implement OpenSpec change rally-083-polish tasks 4.1-4.4. Read design.md and specs/store/spec.md.

Outcome: Try and progress persistence cap each specified free-text field to 3000 runes with head+tail truncation and an explicit marker, while preserving within-cap text verbatim.

Files & scope: Cover internal/store TryRecord Summary and RemainingWork plus internal/progress RunEntry Summary, HandoffEntry Summary, and each Followups string. Reuse or extract the recent-context marker style.

Design: Enforce the durable cap at write boundaries and keep summary.jsonl append-only.

Acceptance: Add store and progress tests for oversized Unicode/rune-aware fields and unchanged small fields, run relevant Go tests, and mark tasks 4.1-4.4 complete in tasks.md.

tasks.title: Harden non-opencode executor summary fallbacks
tasks.description:
Context: Follow up rally-083-polish tasks 3.3-3.5. The verify pass found that  and  still return raw command output on malformed or missing structured results, and  currently treats any non-empty executor summary as trusted final text. That means raw transcripts can still persist through , retry context, and  outside the OpenCode path.

Outcome: Claude/Gemini malformed/no-result paths no longer leak raw transcript output into persisted summaries. Use bounded final text or explicit indicators that satisfy the executor + relay-runner final-snippet semantics without regressing successful structured summaries.

Files & scope: Focus on , , and  only if runner-side discrimination is the cleanest fix. Add focused tests proving malformed/missing structured output cannot persist raw transcripts through retry context, try records, or summary.jsonl.

Acceptance: ok  	github.com/mitchell-wallace/rally/internal/agent	(cached)
ok  	github.com/mitchell-wallace/rally/internal/relay	(cached)
ok  	github.com/mitchell-wallace/rally/internal/progress	(cached) passes and the new tests explicitly cover the raw-transcript regression path for non-opencode executors.

tasks.title: Verify non-opencode summary fallback hardening
tasks.description:
Context: Follow up rally-083-polish final-snippet normalization after the executor-summary gap found in verification. Review the implementation lap that hardens malformed Claude/Gemini fallback summaries so raw command output cannot flow into TryRecord.Summary, retry context, or summary.jsonl through runner normalization.

Verification: Diff only the new follow-up work, run focused executor/runner/progress tests, and confirm malformed or missing structured output now falls back to bounded final text or explicit indicators instead of raw transcripts.

Constraints: Do not rewrite git history. Fix only tiny safe one-liners; otherwise add another focused lap if a substantive gap remains.

tasks.title: Verify final-snippet parsing normalization and persistence
tasks.description:
Context: Review the completed rally-083-polish OpenCode parsing, runner normalization, and persistence-cap slices. Use the openspec-verify-change skill against rally-083-polish, focusing on tasks 3.1-4.4 and the executor, relay-runner, and store specs.

Verification: Identify the intended branch target/base before diffing and identify the first lap/try in this batch; treat earlier branch work as pre-existing. Inspect the diff, run focused tests, and confirm raw transcripts cannot reach Summary through the covered paths.

Constraints: Do not rewrite git history. Fix only tiny safe one-liners; add focused head laps for substantive gaps.

tasks.title: Add inline retry status and run-level final tally
tasks.description:
Context: Implement OpenSpec change rally-083-polish tasks 5.1-5.3. Read design.md and specs/relay-runner/spec.md.

Outcome: Retry progress appears as an inline retry N/M field on the existing live status line without a per-retry console block, and final stats count each run once based on ultimate completion.

Files & scope: Focus on internal/monitor and internal/relay/runner.go with their existing tests.

Design: A retry-then-success run is one pass and zero failures; an exhausted run is one failure. Keep the retry display minimal.

Acceptance: Add tests for status-line retry rendering and both tally scenarios, run relevant Go tests, and mark tasks 5.1-5.3 complete in tasks.md.

tasks.title: Make Rally state commits tolerate ignored operational paths
tasks.description:
Context: Implement OpenSpec change rally-083-polish tasks 6.1-6.3. Read design.md and specs/relay-runner/spec.md.

Outcome: CommitRallyState silently skips operator-gitignored .rally operational paths, continues with other paths, and explicitly tracks .rally/config.toml and .rally/summary.jsonl by default.

Files & scope: Focus on internal/gitx/git.go and tests. This tolerance must not apply to .laps/laps.json, and git add -f is forbidden.

Design: Detect only the specific ignored-path git add condition without masking unrelated git failures.

Acceptance: Add a repository-level test proving one ignored .rally path is skipped while another commits, run relevant Go tests, and mark tasks 6.1-6.3 complete in tasks.md.

tasks.title: Create embedded agent prompt sources and rename user prompts
tasks.description:
Context: Implement OpenSpec change rally-083-polish tasks 7.1-7.4 and 7.7. Read design.md and specs/agent-prompt/spec.md.

Outcome: internal/prompt becomes internal/user_prompt, a new internal/agent_prompt embeds general/ and roles/ markdown sources, embedded role defaults are migrated from current role docs without repeated shared finalize guidance, and AGENTS.md documents the naming convention.

Files & scope: Update all imports for the internal package rename. Do not rewrite operator-owned custom role files as a migration step.

Design: Package names reflect who is being prompted. General finalize and headless guidance must be reusable embedded sources; role markdown must remain role-specific.

Acceptance: Add focused package tests for embedded source loading, run relevant Go tests, and mark tasks 7.1-7.4 and 7.7 complete in tasks.md.

tasks.title: Compose agent prompts with role overrides and shared guidance
tasks.description:
Context: Implement OpenSpec change rally-083-polish task 7.5 using the embedded sources from the prior lap. Read specs/agent-prompt/spec.md and design.md.

Outcome: Normal agent prompts compose shared general snippets, an on-disk role override or embedded role default, then existing task context, while explicit RunOptions.Prompt override semantics remain intact.

Files & scope: Focus on role loading, runner prompt preparation, and internal/agent prompt building. Preserve all existing RunOptions-derived sections and no-backend behavior where applicable.

Design: On-disk .rally/agents/<role>.md overrides only the role slot and never suppresses shared finalize/headless guidance.

Acceptance: Add tests for default loading, override precedence, shared guidance inclusion, prompt ordering, and preserved explicit override/task sections; run relevant Go tests; mark task 7.5 complete in tasks.md.

tasks.title: Add role prompt diagnostics to routes check
tasks.description:
Context: Implement OpenSpec change rally-083-polish tasks 7.6 and the routes-check portion of 7.8. Read specs/agent-prompt/spec.md and design.md.

Outcome: rally routes check always lists detected roles with approximate prompt token counts and emits non-failing advisory overlap diagnostics for custom role files mentioning laps done, laps handoff, laps wrapup, or headless, including embedded general snippets for comparison.

Files & scope: Focus on internal/cli/routes_check.go and tests, using internal/agent_prompt APIs. Leave custom prompt files untouched.

Acceptance: Add tests for role/token output, advisory conflict output, and unchanged success status; run relevant Go tests; mark tasks 7.6 and 7.8 complete in tasks.md once all prompt test expectations are covered.

tasks.title: Verify agent prompt restructure and operational polish
tasks.description:
Context: Review rally-083-polish tasks 2.1-7.8 after the operational and prompt slices. Use the openspec-verify-change skill against rally-083-polish, focusing on agent-prompt composition, routes diagnostics, retry/tally behavior, and gitignore tolerance.

Verification: Identify the intended branch target/base before diffing and the first lap/try in this batch; treat earlier work as pre-existing. Inspect package-rename fallout, run focused and broad tests, and compare implementation to all changed specs.

Constraints: Do not rewrite git history. Fix only tiny safe one-liners; add focused head laps for substantive gaps.

tasks.title: Finish rally 0.8.3 release wiring and full verification
tasks.description:
Context: Complete OpenSpec change rally-083-polish tasks 8.1-8.3 after all implementation laps. Use the openspec-verify-change skill against rally-083-polish and read the release instructions in AGENTS.md.

Outcome: Formatting and the full test suite pass, internal/buildinfo/VERSION is 0.8.3, and a short real relay validates the specified runtime behavior.

Verification: Identify the intended branch target/base before diffing and the first lap/try in this batch; treat earlier branch work as pre-existing. Run just fmt, the full test suite, and a realistic short relay using test-driving-rally guidance. Confirm no false slowing during ordinary reasoning, inline retry N/M, run-level tally, bounded tries.jsonl/summary.jsonl, and wrapup-summary consistency. Mark tasks 8.1-8.3 complete and ensure the full OpenSpec verification passes.

Constraints: Do not create or push a version tag by hand and do not rewrite git history. Add focused head laps for substantive gaps.
